title: General-to-Specific Ordering of Hypotheses in Concept Learning
description: A visual representation of the partial ordering of hypotheses from the most general (anything is an example) to the most specific (nothing is an example), showing how a search through the hypothesis space can be performed.
relationships: The hypotheses are arranged vertically, with the most general at the top and the most specific at the bottom. Arrows point downward from a general hypothesis to its immediate, more specific versions, illustrating the 'more-general-than' relationship.
